model.objectives: minimize used strip_width (X axis) for fixed strip_height (Y axis)

run_b_decision.reason: Run A produced a valid full 276/276 layout with strip_width=1496.15; Run B executed for convergence observation at 2400s.

notes: Upstream Sparrow's SPP model does not natively take margin/spacing/kerf. The Q43 baseline is raw upstream packing. This is documented in the report.
The 1500x6000 strip is the area-equivalent of two 1500x3000 sheets placed end-to-end along the Y axis.
Continuous rotation in upstream is approximated by passing a 16-bin stride of allowed_orientations per item; this matches the own solver's rust/vrs_solver/src/rotation_policy.rs continuous handling.
Run A finished in 1208.18s wall time (1200s solver budget + IO), achieving a 276/276 full placement with strip_width=1496.15mm (residual 3.85mm to the 1500mm bound) and density 0.743.
